title: Adjuster management — roll one leg on adverse move
acceptance_criteria: AdjusterManager implements ManagementHandler interface from T-28
Roll trigger: |current_spot - spot_at_entry| >= personality.params.roll_trigger_points (default 70 points). Checked on each PositionMonitor tick

Roll is modeled as a CLOSE+OPEN cycle (NOT a leg-level operation for M2): close the current straddle with exit_reason='ROLL', open a new straddle at the new current ATM strike at the current straddle_value. Both DB operations run in a single transaction using one captured snapshot timestamp — no P&L gap between close and reopen
parent_trade_id on the new paper_trades row is set to the closed row's id, linking the roll chain
Cumulative P&L of a rolled position chain = sum of net_pnl across all paper_trades rows WHERE id=original_id OR parent_trade_id=original_id (recursive, max 4 hops)

max_open_legs enforcement: count open straddles for this personality (paper_trades WHERE personality_id=x AND status='open') before rolling; if count >= max_open_legs/2 (default 4/2=2), do NOT open the new leg (log warning, treat position as Holder-style instead)
After a roll, the original SL/TSL/TARGET logic applies to the new position from its new entry straddle_value
SL/TSL/TARGET/EOD exits still work on rolled positions identically to Holder — AdjusterManager delegates to the same evaluateTriggers() function for these

Unit tests: roll fires at exactly roll_trigger_points; parent_trade_id linkage is set; max_open_legs cap prevents third roll; transaction atomicity (mock DB that fails on second write — first write must be rolled back)
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
